notes_md:
**Cô** usually means *aunt* — specifically your father’s younger sister. It’s a family noun, so you don’t need a classifier in normal use (cô, không phải *một cô* when talking about your own aunt).

It’s also very common for a *female teacher*, especially in school. Students call their female teacher **cô + name**.

In daily speech, **cô** can be a pronoun for a young woman, or a polite way for a young person to refer to herself when talking to kids.

Close words: **chú** (father’s younger brother), **dì** (mother’s sister).

definitions.en: A term used to refer to a young woman or girl, often used as a respectful address.
definitions.vi: Một thuật ngữ dùng để chỉ một người phụ nữ hoặc cô gái trẻ, thường được sử dụng như một cách xưng hô tôn trọng.
definitions.es: Un término utilizado para referirse a una mujer joven o niña, a menudo usado como una forma de respeto.

exampleSentencesTranslated: This weekend I will visit my aunt's house in the city.
Aunt always teaches me how to cook traditional dishes.
Every Tet holiday, aunt usually gives me small gifts.
notes:
Cô usually means aunt — specifically your father’s younger sister. It’s a family noun, so you don’t need a classifier in normal use (cô, không phải một cô when talking about your own aunt).

It’s also very common for a female teacher, especially in school. Students call their female teacher cô + name.

In daily speech, cô can be a pronoun for a young woman, or a polite way for a young person to refer to herself when talking to kids.

Close words: chú (father’s younger brother), dì (mother’s sister).
exampleSentences: Cuối tuần này em sẽ đến thăm nhà cô ở thành phố.
Cô luôn dạy anh cách nấu những món ăn truyền thống.
Mỗi dịp Tết, cô thường tặng em những món quà nhỏ.
